Wow! After the second SPACE, the character has completely changed! Looking at the image:
- The character is now upside down compared to before
- The green frame is now at the TOP with red blocks
- The white character body is at the BOTTOM
- The character appears to be a "P" shape that's been flipped vertically
This is very revealing! SPACE seems to ROTATE or FLIP the character. Let me check the current positions to understand the transformation better.
The first SPACE moved the character RIGHT by 3 and changed red to gray.
The second SPACE seems to have flipped the character vertically and moved it.
Let me check the current state to understand the pattern.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Nodes after 2nd SPACE:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 5:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check ASCII
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nCharacter area (rows 29-50, cols 36-54):")
for i in range(29, 51):
    print(f"  {i}: {ascii_lines[i][36:54]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
